corpus_integrity_gate.note: HARD GATE. No Doctor Prime experiment may begin until this passes. It caught a real defect on its first full run - one duplicated 512-char context window shared across two distinct documents - which is now globally deduplicated and counted.

honesty: no capability pass exists at any rate on Qwen3-235B
qwen3moe is an Unimplemented stub in the Rust engine: all artifacts are direct-executable in the PYTHON reference forward only, not the .tq serve path

instrument_defect_of_record.damage: S3A layerwise QAT reported +11.4 pct 'held-out' gain; on a split by unique id and segment the same code measures -1.77 pct. The prior was withdrawn and QAT is dead.
instrument_defect_of_record.status: FIXED and GUARDED. corpus_integrity.py splits by document/context-hash and refuses a shared embedding row via assert_layer0_safe; qwen_calibration_corpus now reports n_unique_token_ids and asserts that a bigger token target does not fake diversity.
instrument_defect_of_record.what: qwen_calibration_corpus repeated 12 segments to reach its token target: 1313 positions carried only 540 unique token ids, and min_tokens=20000 still yielded 540.
instrument_defect_of_record.why_it_mattered: layer-0 MoE input is rmsnorm(embed[id]), a pure function of the token id, and the fit/score split was by POSITION - so the same embedding row sat in both halves.

qwen_controller.telegram: alive, per-row, dedup by row_id + checkpoint seal
